overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active and diverse social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, primarily focusing on computational social science, disinformation research, and academic opportunities. With a combined total of 3580 followers and 7 recent posts, the profile demonstrates consistent engagement in academic discourse and professional networking. While LinkedIn boasts the largest audience, Bluesky shows the highest engagement efficiency per post, highlighting distinct strengths for each platform.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to research on coordinated link sharing, information operations, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior across social media platforms.
Discussions and announcements concerning methodological approaches, computational methods for social media analysis, data science, and computational social science.
Announcements of new research grants, hiring for postdoctoral researchers, and opportunities for PhD students within the research team.
Updates on newly published papers and upcoming presentations at academic conferences.

recommendations: Prioritize Bluesky for announcing new research papers and academic opportunities, given its consistently high engagement rate for these types of posts.
Continue to leverage LinkedIn for formal professional news, such as grant awards and job postings, as it reaches the largest audience with strong engagement on these career-oriented topics.

Consider cross-promoting major announcements (e.g., new publications, significant job openings) across all platforms, tailoring the message slightly to best fit each platform's audience and typical engagement style.
For Mastodon, experiment with more direct calls to action, open questions, or links to collaborative opportunities to potentially boost engagement on detailed research discussions.

Regularly use relevant and specific hashtags (e.g., #DisinfoConf2025, #ComputationalSocialScience) across all platforms to increase discoverability and reach within academic and professional communities.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest per-post engagement rate, particularly for announcements of new research publications and calls for academic opportunities. This indicates a highly engaged and responsive community eager for current academic and research-related content.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon serves a niche academic audience, showing consistent engagement for detailed research updates and discussions on methodological approaches. While its overall engagement metrics are comparatively lower, it remains valuable for targeted academic discourse and community building.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the largest follower base and demonstrates strong engagement for formal professional announcements, such as securing new grants and advertising job opportunities. It is the primary platform for broad professional networking and career-focused news.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
